# Auto-Empirical Research Skills (AERS) — five-minute tour

A one-page, on-ramp report so newcomers can decide whether to dig into AERS.
Run `python3 scripts/quickstart.py --markdown` to refresh after `make catalog`.

## What is in this repo

- **69** collections · **1150** skills
- **15** distinct method tags

### Top research stages

- analysis: 139
- writing: 86
- submission: 79
- literature: 62
- reproduction: 58

### Languages represented

- latex: 95
- stata: 48
- python: 46
- r: 22

### Top method tags

- iv: 27
- rdd: 25
- panel-fe: 19
- staggered-did: 17
- bayesian: 13

## Rigor coverage

- **17** method families with closed rigor coverage
- Sample families: ---, Instrumental variables (IV / 2SLS), Regression discontinuity (RDD), Difference-in-differences (2x2), Staggered DiD / TWFE, Event study / pre-trends, Panel fixed effects, Synthetic control …

## License distribution

- MIT: 35 collection(s)
- UNKNOWN - check upstream: 22 collection(s)
- CC-BY-SA-4.0 (repository default): 4 collection(s)
- Mixed (per-skill; see folder): 2 collection(s)
- MIT Non-Commercial: 1 collection(s)
- Apache-2.0: 1 collection(s)
- CC-BY-NC-4.0 (non-commercial): 1 collection(s)
- AGPL-3.0: 1 collection(s)
- CC-BY-SA-4.0: 1 collection(s)
- GPL-3.0: 1 collection(s)

## Where to start

- SKILL.md (catalog router)
- README.md / README-zh-CN.md (overview)
- docs/search.html (faceted skill search)
- docs/SKILL_CATALOG.md (human-readable index)
- docs/CHOOSING_A_SKILL.md (goal→skill routing)
- docs/RIGOR_COVERAGE.md (method-coverage map)

## Next steps

- `make quickstart` — one command to print this tour again.
- `make catalog` — regenerate indexes after adding or removing skills.
- `make check` — full local quality gate before opening a PR.
- `docs/search.html` — faceted skill search (method, stage, language, license).
